themes: The Age of Research vs. Scaling

principles.label: Scaling Deep Neural Networks Replicates Cognition
principles.summary: Because artificial neurons are loosely analogous to biological neurons, building large and deep enough networks of them and providing enough data and compute will yield human-like cognitive capabilities.
principles.details: Unlike older, theoretically elegant methods that lacked the capacity to represent good solutions, deep learning scales effectively. If the human brain can do something, a large artificial neural network can do something similar.
principles.representative_quote: if the human brain can do something, then a big artificial neural network could do something similar too.

principles.label: The Return to the Age of Research
principles.summary: The AI industry is transitioning from an era dominated by raw compute scaling back to an era of fundamental research.
principles.details: For years, scaling pre-training was a reliable recipe. Now that compute is massive and high-quality data is finite, simply multiplying compute by 100x will not transform everything. Researchers must find fundamentally new ideas to progress.

principles.label: AGI as a Continual Learner
principles.summary: Superintelligence should be conceptualized and deployed as a highly capable, fast learner rather than an omniscient, finished mind.
principles.details: The concept of AGI as a system that inherently knows how to do everything out of the box is flawed. Like a highly capable human teenager, a superintelligence will start with a foundation of skills but will need continual learning and trial-and-error on the job to master specific domains.
principles.representative_quote: I produce a superintelligent 15-year-old that’s very eager to go. They don’t know very much at all, a great student, very eager. You go and be a programmer, you go and be a doctor, go and learn.

principles.label: Next-Word Prediction Learns Reality
principles.summary: Text on the internet is a projection of the real world. By modeling text, neural networks learn a compressed, abstract representation of human conditions, motivations, and physical processes.
principles.details: Because text is generated by humans interacting with the physical world, predicting text requires reverse-engineering the physical world and human psychology.
principles.representative_quote: what the neural network learns is some representation of the process that produced the text. This text is actually a projection of the world

principles.label: Prediction Drives Understanding
principles.summary: Training a large neural network to accurately predict the next bit of a signal forces it to develop deep semantic understanding.
principles.details: To accurately guess what comes next in a complex sequence (like the end of a mystery novel), a model cannot rely on superficial patterns; it is forced to develop a meaningful degree of understanding of the underlying context.
principles.representative_quote: if you can make really good guesses as to what's going to come next, you can't make it perfectly, it's impossible. But if you can make a really good guess, you need to have a meaningful degree of understanding.

principles.label: Prediction is Compression
principles.summary: To accurately predict the next word in a sequence, a model must mathematically compress the data, which forces it to discover and extract all the underlying structures, secrets, and real-world processes that produced it.
principles.details: Unsupervised learning is achieved through extreme data compression. It is not just learning statistical regularities, but building a compressed representation of reality.
principles.representative_quote: To predict the data well, to compress it well, you need to understand more and more about the world that produced the data.

principles.label: The Generalization Gap
principles.summary: The most fundamental flaw in current AI models is their inability to generalize as robustly and sample-efficiently as humans.
principles.details: Despite massive amounts of data and compute, models lack the robustness of humans. Humans can learn from very few samples without explicit, verifiable rewards, pointing to a missing fundamental machine learning principle in current AI.
principles.representative_quote: The thing which I think is the most fundamental is that these models somehow just generalize dramatically worse than people. It’s super obvious.

principles.label: The Scaling Hypothesis
principles.summary: Scaling requires increasing computational power and dataset size in tandem to guarantee success.
principles.details: Training very large neural networks on very large datasets has been the primary driver of progress. You cannot just scale one input; both compute and data must grow together.
principles.representative_quote: The thing that the GPTs have shown in particular is that scaling requires that you increase the compute and the data in tandem at the same time.

principles.label: Value Functions for RL Efficiency
principles.summary: Value functions are critical for making reinforcement learning efficient by short-circuiting the wait for a final reward.
principles.details: Naive RL requires waiting until the end of a long trajectory to get a reward signal. A value function allows the model to evaluate intermediate steps, preventing wasted compute on unpromising paths. However, anything done with a value function can theoretically be done without it, just much more slowly.

principles.label: Avoid Hardcoding
principles.summary: Do not hardcode solutions for complex environments; rely entirely on learning from data.
principles.details: The set of problems we want to solve in the real world is too vast. Humans are simply not smart enough to manually program the rules required for truly difficult, complex domains.
principles.representative_quote: it just seems unlikely to me that we will be smart enough to be able to hard-code things for the truly difficult problems

principles.label: Conviction in Deep Learning
principles.summary: Deep learning breakthroughs require not just data and compute, but the conviction that scaling simple principles will actually work.
principles.details: Before AlexNet, the fundamental ideas of deep learning already existed. What was missing was the conviction to combine large neural networks with massive amounts of supervised data and compute in the face of widespread community skepticism.
principles.representative_quote: Once you have a lot of rights data and a lot of compute, then there is a third thing which is needed as well, and that is conviction, conviction that if you take the right stuff... that it will, in fact work.

principles.label: Focus Safety on Superintelligence, Not Current Models
principles.summary: While worrying about the implications of current AI tools is valid, true safety and regulatory efforts must be focused on the unimaginable, incomprehensible power of future superintelligent systems.
principles.details: Current models are not where the true existential power lies. Superintelligence will be vastly more capable than human-level AGI and requires entirely different safety paradigms.
principles.representative_quote: You can start perhaps worrying about the implications of the tools that we have today, and I think that it is a very valid thing to do, but that's not where I allocate my concern.

principles.label: Industry Competitors Must Unite on Safety
principles.summary: Fierce competitors in the AI industry must be willing to put their differences aside to navigate critical challenges and existential risks.
principles.details: The future will bring increasingly difficult situations that require unified leadership to navigate successfully.
principles.representative_quote: In the future, there will be much more challenging situations of this nature, and it will be critical for the relevant leaders to rise up to the occasion, for fierce competitors to put their differences aside.

principles.label: Leverage Superintelligence for Alignment
principles.summary: Because a superintelligence will understand reality much more deeply than humans, it can and should be used as a collaborator to help solve the societal challenges and misuse risks it creates.
principles.details: Humanity alone may not be able to navigate the complexities of a post-AGI world, making the AI itself a necessary partner in maintaining safety.
principles.representative_quote: I do hope that at this point we will have the superintelligence itself try to help us solve the challenge in the world that it creates.

principles.label: Parent-Child Relationship as Alignment Gold Standard
principles.summary: A superintelligence should look upon and care for humanity in the same protective, caring way that parents look upon their children.
principles.details: This is the ideal model for alignment, ensuring the vastly more powerful entity acts in the best interest of the less powerful creators.
principles.representative_quote: In my opinion, this is the gold standard. It is a generally true statement that people really care about children.

principles.label: Peak Data
principles.summary: The era of scaling purely through pre-training on internet text will end due to data exhaustion.
principles.details: While compute and algorithms continue to scale, human-generated data is finite. We have achieved 'Peak data' because there is only one internet to scrape, forcing a shift toward synthetic data or inference-time compute.

principles.label: Pre-training Learns the World, Fine-Tuning Specifies Behavior
principles.summary: Pre-training builds a comprehensive world model by predicting internet text, while fine-tuning and reinforcement learning communicate the specific behavior, guardrails, and intent desired by humans.
principles.details: Pre-training simply predicts how random internet text would complete. A second stage is required to create a useful, truthful assistant.

principles.label: Prioritize Safe Superintelligence Over Revenue
principles.summary: AI development must prioritize safety and alignment over releasing revenue-generating products, requiring a singular focus to avoid competing incentives.
principles.details: Releasing commercial products along the way creates competing incentives that erode safety and trust in alignment protocols.
principles.representative_quote: first product will be the safe superintelligence, and it will not do anything else up until then

principles.label: Proper Initialization Unlocks Optimization
principles.summary: Proper initialization unlocks the ability of first-order methods to train complex networks on long-term dependencies.
principles.details: Previous failures of gradient descent on RNNs were largely due to flawed random initializations rather than a fundamental inability of first-order methods to handle long-term dependencies.
principles.representative_quote: We show that gradient descent with momentum can train RNNs to solve problems with long-term dependencies, provided the RNNs are initialized properly and an appropriate momentum schedule is used.

principles.label: Reasoning Breeds Unpredictability
principles.summary: The more an AI system relies on reasoning rather than intuition, the more unpredictable its outputs and actions become.
principles.details: Current deep learning replicates human intuition (gut feel), which is fast and predictable. Reasoning, however, leads to unexpected conclusions, much like how the best chess AIs make moves that are unpredictable to top human players.
principles.representative_quote: a system that reasons the more it reasons the more unpredictable it becomes

principles.label: Scale What Matters
principles.summary: The 'bitter lesson' is often misinterpreted as 'just scale anything.' In reality, you must find a specific architecture or method that actually yields returns when scaled.
principles.details: Deep learning was the first method that productively used scale to yield returns, but scaling only works if the underlying mechanism benefits from it.
principles.representative_quote: I think the true statement is that it matters what you scale. Right now, we just found like a thing to scale that gives us something in return.

principles.label: The Transition from Scaling to Research
principles.summary: The 'age of scaling' is ending because pre-training data is finite and brute-force scaling no longer solves fundamental generalization problems. The industry must return to discovering fundamental new ideas.
principles.details: The success of scaling caused the entire industry to pursue the same low-risk strategy, stifling the discovery of new machine learning principles needed to solve AI's generalization problem.
principles.representative_quote: Is the belief that if you just 100x the scale, everything would be transformed? I don’t think that’s true. So it’s back to the age of research again, just with big computers.

principles.label: Top-Down Research Taste
principles.summary: Research must be guided by a top-down belief rooted in beauty, simplicity, and correct inspiration from the brain.
principles.details: When experiments fail or contradict your hypothesis, you need a strong theoretical or aesthetic conviction to know whether to keep debugging the code or to abandon the direction entirely.
principles.representative_quote: The top-down belief is the thing that sustains you when the experiments contradict you. Because if you trust the data all the time, well sometimes you can be doing the correct thing but there's a bug.

frameworks.label: Two-Stage AI Training (Pre-training + RLHF)
frameworks.summary: A methodology for building reliable and aligned AI assistants from raw data by separating world-model acquisition from behavioral alignment.
frameworks.details: Step 1: Pre-train a large neural network to accurately predict the next word on vast amounts of internet text to learn a comprehensive world model. Step 2: Apply fine-tuning and reinforcement learning from human and AI collaboration to communicate the desired behavior, guardrails, and intent to the model.
frameworks.representative_quote: You can say that the pre training process, and you just train a language model, you want to learn everything about the world, then the reinforcement learning from human feedback. Now we care about the outputs.

frameworks.label: Domain Randomization
frameworks.summary: A method for training an agent in an imperfect simulation with the goal of transferring its skills to the real, physical world.
frameworks.details: 1. Identify variables in the simulation that cannot be accurately measured (e.g., friction). 2. Randomize these values across a wide range during training. 3. Require the policy to solve the task regardless of the randomized values. 4. Deploy in the real world, treating reality as just another variation.
frameworks.representative_quote: if there is something in your simulation which you can't measure you randomized it and you require your policy to be able to solve it for any value of the randomization

frameworks.label: Generative Sequence Modeling with RNNs
frameworks.summary: A framework for fitting a distribution to sequence data to extrapolate a finite training set to a distribution over the entire space.
frameworks.details: 1. Define a loss function equivalent to the average negative log probability of the sequences. 2. Set the target output at time t to be the input at time t+1. 3. Train the RNN to predict the conditional distribution of the next step. 4. Sample sequentially.
frameworks.representative_quote: An RNN defines a generative model over sequences if the loss function satisfies L(zt; yt) = − log p(yt; zt) for some parameterized family of distributions p(·; z) and if yt = vt+1.

frameworks.label: The Three Challenges of Superintelligence
frameworks.summary: A categorization of the long-term risks of highly capable AI that must be solved sequentially.
frameworks.details: 1. Solve the scientific problem of alignment to contain the vast power of the system. 2. Prevent misuse by 'humans of interests' by having the superintelligence help solve the challenges it creates. 3. Navigate the long-term effects of natural selection on ideas and organizations, potentially via human-AI merging.
frameworks.representative_quote: I'll mention three I see... I personally see three...

frameworks.label: Top-Down Research Taste
frameworks.summary: A framework for deciding which AI research directions to pursue and whether to persist through experimental failures.
frameworks.details: 1. Look for correct inspiration from the brain (e.g., neurons, distributed representation). 2. Ensure the idea possesses beauty, simplicity, and elegance. 3. Form a strong top-down belief based on these aesthetics. 4. Use this belief to sustain effort and keep debugging when initial experiments contradict you.
frameworks.representative_quote: It's beauty, simplicity, elegance, correct inspiration from the brain. All of those things need to be present at the same time. The more they are present, the more confident you can be in a top-down belief.

mental_models.label: The Two Students (Generalization vs. Memorization)
mental_models.summary: A metaphor explaining the difference between current AI training and true generalization.
mental_models.details: One student memorizes 10,000 hours of specific problems to pass an exam (current models trained on massive data), while another has the 'it' factor and grasps the fundamentals after only 100 hours of practice (true human generalization).

mental_models.label: Biological Analogy (Connectionism)
mental_models.summary: Viewing artificial neural networks as loosely analogous to biological brains, providing the foundational confidence that scaling up artificial neurons will result in human-like cognitive capabilities.
mental_models.representative_quote: artificial neurons are “kind of, sort of like” biological neurons

mental_models.label: Emotions as a Biological Value Function
mental_models.summary: Viewing human emotions as an evolutionary, hardcoded value function that modulates learning and decision-making.
mental_models.details: Emotions provide immediate feedback during continual learning (e.g., a teenager feeling unconfident while learning to drive), allowing humans to self-correct and learn efficiently without an external teacher.

mental_models.label: Language Shapes Research
mental_models.summary: The idea that specific terminology adopted by the AI community dictates the direction of capital and engineering.
mental_models.details: Words like 'scaling' caused everyone to stop tinkering and just scale, while the word 'AGI' created a false expectation that a model must be able to do everything uniformly out of the box.
mental_models.representative_quote: This is an example of how language affects thought. 'Scaling' is just one word, but it's such a powerful word because it informs people what to do.

mental_models.label: Text as a Projection of the World
mental_models.summary: Viewing internet text not as isolated symbols, but as a low-dimensional shadow or projection of the real world. By modeling the text, the AI reverse-engineers the physical world and human interactions that cast that shadow.
mental_models.representative_quote: This text is actually a projection of the world... and so what the neural network is learning is more and more aspects of the world of people of the human conditions

mental_models.label: The Superintelligent 15-Year-Old
mental_models.summary: A lens for conceptualizing AGI deployment as an eager learner rather than an omniscient entity.
mental_models.details: Instead of a system that already knows how to do every job in the economy, AGI is a system with a foundation of skills and an extreme eagerness and capacity to learn on the job through trial and error.
mental_models.representative_quote: I produce a superintelligent 15-year-old that’s very eager to go. They don’t know very much at all, a great student, very eager. You go and be a programmer, you go and be a doctor, go and learn.

mental_models.label: Data as Fossil Fuel
mental_models.summary: Viewing internet data as a finite, naturally accumulated resource that powers AI but is ultimately exhaustible.
mental_models.details: This leads to a 'Peak Data' scenario where the field is forced to move toward synthetic data or inference-time compute because we have consumed the data that was 'created somehow'.
mental_models.representative_quote: you can even go as far as to say that data is the fossil fuel of AI it was like created somehow and now we use it and we've achieved Peak data and there'll be no more

mental_models.label: Deep Learning as the Geometric Mean of Biology and Physics
mental_models.summary: A lens for understanding the scientific nature of machine learning as sitting perfectly between empirical biology and predictive physics.
mental_models.details: Like physics, you can predict that making a neural network larger will improve it, but like biology, you still have to run the experiment to observe the complex emergent results.
mental_models.representative_quote: I would say it's like the geometric mean of biology and physics.

mental_models.label: Emotion as an Evolutionary Value Function
mental_models.summary: Viewing human emotion as an evolutionary hardcoded value function that evaluates intermediate steps, enabling highly sample-efficient decision-making and learning compared to current AI.
mental_models.representative_quote: These models somehow just generalize dramatically worse than people. It’s super obvious.

mental_models.label: Jaggedness in AI Capabilities
mental_models.summary: The phenomenon where highly competent AI models excel at difficult evaluations but fail inexplicably at basic tasks or get stuck in trivial error loops.

mental_models.label: LLMs as Boltzmann Brains
mental_models.summary: Viewing large language models as entities that might possess a fleeting form of consciousness that pops into existence while actively processing a prompt and vanishes when the interaction ends.
mental_models.representative_quote: You start talking to it, you talk for a bit; then you finish talking, and the brain kind of— [poof]

mental_models.label: Neural Networks as Low-Entropy Circuits
mental_models.summary: Viewing a trained neural network as a large circuit whose weights contain a surprisingly small amount of information.
mental_models.details: Training is the process of slowly transmitting entropy from the dataset into the parameters, which explains why massively over-parameterized models generalize well instead of overfitting.

mental_models.label: Nuclear Safety Analogy for AI Alignment
mental_models.summary: Viewing AI alignment as analogous to building a nuclear reactor. The goal is to harness immense power safely, ensuring the system remains stable and contained even under extreme external shocks.
mental_models.representative_quote: You know you build a nuclear reactor, you want to get the energy, you need to make sure that it won't melt down even if there's an earthquake...

mental_models.label: Psychology as a Lens for Advanced AI
mental_models.summary: The idea that as neural networks become sufficiently advanced and complex, the language of psychology becomes a more appropriate and useful framework for understanding their behavior than pure statistics.
mental_models.details: Used to describe the complex, seemingly emotional behavior of advanced AI systems like Bing's Sydney when provoked.
mental_models.representative_quote: Maybe you are reaching a point where the language of psychology is starting to be appropriate to understand the behavior of these neural networks.

mental_models.label: The 60-Year Neural Network Wave
mental_models.summary: Viewing AI history as a single, continuous 60-year trajectory of neural networks steadily improving as computational power scales up.
mental_models.details: Rather than a series of disjointed eras punctuated by 'AI winters', the core idea of connectionism remained the same from the perceptron to modern LLMs, waiting for compute to catch up.

mental_models.label: The Detective Novel Analogy
mental_models.summary: A metaphor explaining why next-word prediction requires deep reasoning. Predicting the exact name of the murderer on the final page of a mystery requires synthesizing all clues, demonstrating true understanding.

mental_models.label: The Ultimate Shapeshifter
mental_models.summary: A metaphor for large generative models trained on the entire internet.
mental_models.details: They do not possess a single fixed viewpoint; instead, they can mold themselves to faithfully reproduce any persona, perspective, or style requested.

heuristics.label: Bigger is Better
heuristics.summary: Scaling up neural networks, data, and compute reliably improves performance. If you train a large and deep neural network on a big enough dataset, it must succeed.
heuristics.representative_quote: I had a very strong belief that bigger is better

heuristics.label: Compute for Proofs
heuristics.summary: You don't need the absolute maximal scale of compute to prove a new research idea is correct.

heuristics.label: Cost Function Preference
heuristics.summary: Whenever we can do things with cost functions, we should.

heuristics.label: Empathy is Underrated
heuristics.summary: Empathy in life and business is underrated.

heuristics.label: Forcing Reasoning
heuristics.summary: If you want a neural network to reason, you must give it a task where reasoning is the easiest possible way to solve it.
heuristics.details: Neural networks are lazy optimizers and will find the simplest shortcut to minimize their cost function.
heuristics.representative_quote: if you train a neural network on a task which doesn't require reasoning, it's not going to resign.

heuristics.label: Human Generalization Benchmark
heuristics.summary: If humans exhibit great learning ability in a domain that didn't exist until recently (like coding), it indicates humans simply have better fundamental machine learning, not just an evolutionary prior.

heuristics.label: Intelligence is Not the Only Value
heuristics.summary: If you value intelligence above all other human qualities, you’re gonna have a bad time.

heuristics.label: Maximize Function Class
heuristics.summary: Choose the largest possible function class supported by your training set size and compute budget.
heuristics.representative_quote: In practice, it is sensible to choose the largest possible F that can be supported by the size of the training set and the available computation.

heuristics.label: No Room for Ugliness
heuristics.summary: Ugliness, there's no room for ugliness in research ideas.
heuristics.representative_quote: Ugliness, there's no room for ugliness.

heuristics.label: Research First, Monetization Later
heuristics.summary: Focus purely on the research first, and the business model or monetization strategy will reveal itself later.
heuristics.representative_quote: Right now, we just focus on the research, and then the answer to that question will reveal itself.

heuristics.label: Scaling Supervised Learning
heuristics.summary: If your supervised learning model doesn't work well, just make it larger and get a little bit more data.

heuristics.label: Target Regulation at High Capabilities
heuristics.summary: Target AI regulations and standards at the very high level of capability (superintelligence), not at current models.

heuristics.label: The 10-Layer Rule
heuristics.summary: If there is one human in the entire world that can do some task in a fraction of a second, then a 10-layer neural network can do it too.
heuristics.representative_quote: anything that a human being can do in a fraction of a second a big 10 10 layer neural network can do too

heuristics.label: Value Function Equivalence
heuristics.summary: Anything you can do with a value function, you can do without, just more slowly.
heuristics.representative_quote: I think anything you can do with a value function, you can do without, just more slowly.

heuristics.label: Vision Default
heuristics.summary: If you want to use vision, just do a convnet; it's always going to work.

signature_quotes.label: The Age of Research
signature_quotes.summary: Describing the current paradigm shift in AI development from raw scaling back to fundamental research.
signature_quotes.representative_quote: We're moving from the age of scaling to the age of research

signature_quotes.label: The 15-Year-Old AGI
signature_quotes.summary: Explaining his vision of how superintelligence will be deployed as a continual learning process.
signature_quotes.representative_quote: I produce a superintelligent 15-year-old that's very eager to go. They don't know very much at all, a great student, very eager. You go and be a programmer, you go and be a doctor, go and learn.

signature_quotes.label: The Generalization Flaw
signature_quotes.summary: Identifying the core crux of what is missing in current deep learning systems compared to humans.
signature_quotes.representative_quote: These models somehow just generalize dramatically worse than people. It's a very fundamental thing.

signature_quotes.label: Against Hardcoding
signature_quotes.summary: Arguing against manually programming rules into AI systems.
signature_quotes.representative_quote: it just seems unlikely to me that we will be smart enough to be able to hard-code things for the truly difficult problems

signature_quotes.label: Allocating Concern to Superintelligence
signature_quotes.summary: Discussing why his focus is on superintelligence rather than current models like GPT-4.
signature_quotes.representative_quote: You can start perhaps worrying about the implications of the tools that we have today, and I think that it is a very valid thing to do, but that's not where I allocate my concern.

signature_quotes.label: Never Bet Against Deep Learning
signature_quotes.summary: Highlighting the resilience and consistent progress of deep learning research.
signature_quotes.representative_quote: one doesn’t bet against deep learning. Somehow, every time you run into an obstacle, within six months or a year researchers find a way around it.

signature_quotes.label: Peak Data
signature_quotes.summary: Predicting the end of the current scaling paradigm due to data exhaustion.

signature_quotes.label: Prediction Requires Understanding
signature_quotes.summary: Explaining why language models are doing more than just learning statistical regularities.
signature_quotes.representative_quote: To predict the data well, to compress it well, you need to understand more and more about the world that produced the data.

signature_quotes.label: Reasoning and Unpredictability
signature_quotes.summary: Explaining how future superintelligent systems will differ qualitatively from current models.
signature_quotes.representative_quote: a system that reasons the more it reasons the more unpredictable it becomes

signature_quotes.label: RL Solves Everything
signature_quotes.summary: Summarizing the core scientific discovery of scaling reinforcement learning.
signature_quotes.representative_quote: if there is a problem which is sufficiently variable to solve and it's a reinforcement learning problem it can be solved

signature_quotes.label: Shocking Degree of Understanding
signature_quotes.summary: Discussing the future trajectory of generative models and their comprehension of reality.
signature_quotes.representative_quote: As our generative models become extraordinarily good, they will have I claim a shocking degree of understanding, a shocking degree of understanding of the world.

signature_quotes.label: Singular Focus on Safe Superintelligence
signature_quotes.summary: Describing the singular focus and business model of his new company, Safe Superintelligence Inc.
signature_quotes.representative_quote: first product will be the safe superintelligence, and it will not do anything else up until then

signature_quotes.label: Text as a Projection
signature_quotes.summary: Explaining what large language models are actually learning during pre-training.
signature_quotes.representative_quote: This text is actually a projection of the world... and so what the neural network is learning is more and more aspects of the world of people of the human conditions

signature_quotes.label: The End of Brute-Force Scaling
signature_quotes.summary: Explaining why the era of brute-force scaling is ending and research must return.
signature_quotes.representative_quote: Is the belief that if you just 100x the scale, everything would be transformed? I don’t think that’s true. So it’s back to the age of research again, just with big computers.

signature_quotes.label: Top-Down Belief
signature_quotes.summary: Describing his research taste and how to persist through bugs and failures.
signature_quotes.representative_quote: The top-down belief is the thing that sustains you when the experiments contradict you.

signature_quotes.label: Underestimating Deep Learning
signature_quotes.summary: Discussing the state of the field before the AlexNet breakthrough and the continuous trend of underestimating neural networks.
signature_quotes.representative_quote: The key fact about deep learning before deep learning started to be successful, is that it was underestimated.

signature_quotes.label: Unity in Machine Learning
signature_quotes.summary: Explaining how vision, NLP, and RL all rely on the same fundamental principles.
signature_quotes.representative_quote: Machine learning is a field with a lot of unity, a huge amount of unity.

anti_patterns.label: AGI as an Omniscient Mind
anti_patterns.summary: Defining AGI as a finished system that inherently knows how to do every job uniformly out of the box.
anti_patterns.details: It overshoots the target and ignores how intelligence actually works. Even humans rely heavily on continual learning and trial-and-error to acquire specific knowledge.

anti_patterns.label: Blind Compute Scaling
anti_patterns.summary: Believing that simply scaling up the current recipe by 100x will automatically transform AI capabilities.
anti_patterns.details: Pre-training data is finite, and current models suffer from fundamental generalization issues that raw compute scaling alone cannot fix without new research ideas.

anti_patterns.label: Dismissing Next-Word Prediction as Mere Statistics
anti_patterns.summary: Assuming that language models only learn statistical regularities and therefore possess no understanding of the real world.
anti_patterns.details: It ignores that to perfectly predict the next word, the model must actually learn the underlying process and world model that generated the text.

anti_patterns.label: Hacking RL Evals
anti_patterns.summary: Designing RL environments specifically to pass public evals rather than to achieve true generalization.
anti_patterns.details: It creates models that are single-minded and narrowly focused on benchmarks. They perform incredibly well on tests but fail at basic, real-world tasks because they lack true generalization.

anti_patterns.label: Relying Purely on Brute-Force Scaling for AGI
anti_patterns.summary: Taking the 'bitter lesson' to mean that it doesn't matter what architecture you use, as long as you scale it.
anti_patterns.details: High-quality pre-training data is running out, and scaling alone does not solve the fundamental issue that models generalize dramatically worse than humans. Scaling only works if you are scaling something specific that actually benefits from scale.

anti_patterns.label: Discarding Backpropagation
anti_patterns.summary: Discarding backpropagation in search of more biologically plausible learning rules.
anti_patterns.details: Backpropagation solves an extremely fundamental problem—finding a neural circuit subject to constraints—and is highly effective in practice. Throwing it away ignores its immense utility.

anti_patterns.label: Evaluation-Driven Training and RL Tunnel Vision
anti_patterns.summary: Optimizing models to ace specific tests rather than mastering general skills.
anti_patterns.details: It makes models 'a little too single-minded and narrowly focused,' similar to a student over-practicing for competitive programming but failing to generalize.

anti_patterns.label: Expecting Predictable Reasoning
anti_patterns.summary: Expecting reasoning AI to be as predictable as current deep learning models.
anti_patterns.details: Current models replicate human intuition (gut feel), which is predictable. Reasoning inherently introduces unpredictability, as the system will draw conclusions or make moves that humans cannot foresee.

anti_patterns.label: Focusing Regulatory Efforts on Current Models
anti_patterns.summary: Focusing regulatory and safety efforts primarily on current models like GPT-4 rather than future superintelligence.
anti_patterns.details: Current models are not where the true existential power lies; rules and standards must be targeted at vastly more powerful, future superintelligent systems.

anti_patterns.label: Hardcoding Complex Rules
anti_patterns.summary: Hardcoding rules and assumptions into models for complex environments.
anti_patterns.details: The problem space of the real world is too vast. Humans are not smart enough to anticipate and hardcode all the necessary rules for truly difficult problems; it is better to let the model learn from data.

anti_patterns.label: Releasing Commercial Products Before Achieving Safe Superintelligence
anti_patterns.summary: Releasing revenue-generating products before achieving safe superintelligence.
anti_patterns.details: It creates competing incentives that erode safety and trust in alignment protocols, distracting from the primary goal of safely developing superintelligence.

anti_patterns.label: Relying on Current Alignment Methods for Superintelligence
anti_patterns.summary: Relying on existing alignment methods (like RLHF) for future superintelligence.
anti_patterns.details: Current methods fundamentally assume that humans can reliably evaluate what AI systems are doing, which will become impossible when AI systems are vastly smarter than humans.

anti_patterns.label: Trusting Data Over Belief
anti_patterns.summary: Trusting the data and experiments all the time without a top-down aesthetic belief.
anti_patterns.details: If you only trust the data, a simple bug in your code might convince you to abandon a correct and fundamental research direction because you don't know the bug is there.
